visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
It is applicable in case you are travelling for the purpose of study. This visa is given for a period of the stay up to 90 days within 180 days

## Visa Fees
The fees must be paid at the office counter when submitting the application. Visa fee is payable in cash. Please note that this is non-refundable, whatever the consul's decision.
In accordance with article 16 of REGULATION (EC) No 810/2009 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL of 13 July 2009 establishing a Community Code on Visas (Visa Code) (OJ L 243,
                                                                        15.9.2009, p. 1) basic visa fee is EUR 80. Children aged six to under 12 pay a visa fee of EUR 40.

[Table:   Student visa]

[Table:   Student visa]

## Visa fee exemptions
- Children under 6 years of age;
- Foreigners who are spouses of Polish nationals;
- Foreigners who are spouses of EU citizens;
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training;
- Researchers from third countries traveling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council
                                                                            of the September 28, 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries traveling within the community for the
                                                                            purpose of carrying out scientific research;
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.

## Documents Required
- Registered application form
Form must be filled out on-line, printed out and signed personally by the applicant (for minors, the application shall be signed personally by at least one of the parents or a
                                                                                legal guardian while submitting the application).
All entries should be properly filled out - no blanks.
- 1 passport size colour photograph
Size 35 x 45 mm.
White background.
Not older than 6 months.
Printed on high quality paper.
Complying with ICAO guidelines (International Civil Aviation Organization)
- Original passport
Must be valid at least 3 months calculating from the date of intended departure from the Schengen area.
Must contain at least two blank visa pages, excluding the endorsements pages.
Not older than 10 years (extended passports are not accepted).
- - - Travel health insurance certificate and its copy
The sum insured has to exceed 30.000 Euro.
Only a policy issued by an insurance company based in Europe or listed insurance companies are accepted by the Embassies of the Schengen Countries in Manila.
The policy shall be valid for all Schengen area states.
The policy shall cover at least the entire period of the first trip.
List of Schengen Insurance Companies (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6grCRUd0t5zxTXa84TeHME/92037f344e34bc81b5988d3df58eedd9/list-of-schengen-insurance-companies-20-oct-2022.pdf)
- - (if applicable)
- - (if available)
- Documents confirming financial resources for study in Poland
e.g. bank statements presenting the transaction/movement of the last 6 months and employment letter of applicant. if the costs are covered by the sponsor: sponsor's 6 month bank
                                                                                account statement, a notary sponsorship letter, a document proving family ties.
- - - Copy of Alien Certificate Registration (ACR)/re-entry permit - the document shall be valid for at least 3 months after expiry of the visa.
For residents holding non-Philippine passport only.
- * Information about Refunding Student Fees in Case of Receiving a Visa Refusal in Order to Start or Continue Studies (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7Bvmr44waGCnJMWHm8I5fy/8d5a64bb017975874449ef430dc19cd9/information-about-refunding-student-fees.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
All photos must meet the requirements listed below. If these requirements have not been met, the applicant will have to deliver a new photo that meets the requirements.
FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
- Size of photograph: 3.5 X 4.5 cm
- Sharp and in color, printed on high-quality paper with a white background
- Not older than 6 months, enabling identification of applying person
- "En face" - face straight to the objective, both sides of the face need to be visible
- Photograph must present a person from top of head to shoulders
- Size of the head should cover 70-80% of vertical length of a photo
- Head must be uncovered and unveiled, face must not be covered by hair, eyes must be visible, mouth must be shut
IF YOU WEAR GLASSES:
- Glasses must not be dark
- Eyes must be visible through frames
- Glasses must not reflect the light
IF YOU WEAR RELIGIOUS HEAD COVERS:
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- Face must not be shadowed by head cover
The photo requirements are the same for children as for adults. 
Photo requirements visa (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5BqFWHU1i7jlmQZm04A0FQ/9dafaa079d796779b33dbf8c7aad2a22/Photo_requirements_visa.pdf)

## Processing Time
The standard processing time for visa processing is 15 calendar days.
The processing time might be extended for citizens of certain countries or specific categories of travelers due to mandatory visa consultation. Third countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation is published on the website of the European Commission - click here (https://ec.europa.eu/home-affairs/system/files/2022-04/Annex%2016%20-%20prior%20consultation_en.pdf).

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
It is applicable in case you are travelling for the purpose of joining a vessel. This visa is given for a period of the stay up to 90 days within 180 days.

## Visa Fees
The fees must be paid at the office counter when submitting the application. Visa fee is payable in cash. Please note that this is non-refundable, whatever the consul's decision.
In accordance with article 16 of REGULATION (EC) No 810/2009 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL of 13 July 2009 establishing a Community Code on Visas (Visa Code) (OJ L 243,
                                                                        15.9.2009, p. 1) basic visa fee is EUR 80. Children aged six to under 12 pay a visa fee of EUR 40.

[Table:   Seafarers]

[Table:   Seafarers]

## Visa fee exemptions
- Children under 6 years of age;
- Foreigners who are spouses of Polish nationals;
- Foreigners who are spouses of EU citizens;
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training;
- Researchers from third countries traveling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council
                                                                            of the September 28, 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries traveling within the community for the
                                                                            purpose of carrying out scientific research;
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.

## Documents Required
- Registered application form
Form must be filled out on-line, printed out and signed personally by the applicant (for minors, the application shall be signed personally by at least one of the parents or a
                                                                                legal guardian while submitting the application).
All entries should be properly filled out - no blanks.
- 1 passport size colour photograph
Size 35 x 45 mm.
White background.
Not older than 6 months.
Printed on high quality paper.
Complying with ICAO guidelines (International Civil Aviation Organization)
- Original passport
Must be valid at least 3 months calculating from the date of intended departure from the Schengen area.
Must contain at least two blank visa pages, excluding the endorsements pages.
Not older than 10 years (extended passports are not accepted).
- - - (if applicable, original and copy of old seaman’s books)
- Travel health insurance certificate and its copy
The sum insured has to exceed 30.000 Euro.
Only a policy issued by an insurance company based in Europe or listed insurance companies are accepted by the Embassies of the Schengen Countries in Manila.
The policy shall be valid for all Schengen area states.
The policy shall cover at least the entire period of the first trip.
List of Schengen Insurance Companies (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6grCRUd0t5zxTXa84TeHME/92037f344e34bc81b5988d3df58eedd9/list-of-schengen-insurance-companies-20-oct-2022.pdf)
- - - Invitation letter from the Polish shipping agency
It must state whether the organizers cover the expenses of living during the applicant’s transit. The following information must also be included in the letter:
Biodata of the seafarer (full name, place and date of birth);
Passport and seaman’s books numbers with date of issue and period of validity;
The sailor’s position on the vessel (in case of a group of sailors, this information can be included in a list signed, sealed, and attached to the invitation letter);
Name of vessel;
Date and airport of entry into the Schengen area;
Port of boarding in Poland;
Duration of the contract;
- Itinerary that the seafarer will follow to arrive in the Schengen area then the port of boarding and who will be in charge to transport him to the final destination to board
                                  the vessel;
- 
## Photo Specifications
All photos must meet the requirements listed below. If these requirements have not been met, the applicant will have to deliver a new photo that meets the requirements.
FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
- Size of photograph: 3.5 X 4.5 cm
- Sharp and in color, printed on high-quality paper with a white background
- Not older than 6 months, enabling identification of applying person
- "En face" - face straight to the objective, both sides of the face need to be visible
- Photograph must present a person from top of head to shoulders
- Size of the head should cover 70-80% of vertical length of a photo
- Head must be uncovered and unveiled, face must not be covered by hair, eyes must be visible, mouth must be shut
IF YOU WEAR GLASSES:
- Glasses must not be dark
- Eyes must be visible through frames
- Glasses must not reflect the light
IF YOU WEAR RELIGIOUS HEAD COVERS:
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- Face must not be shadowed by head cover
The photo requirements are the same for children as for adults. 
Photo requirements visa (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5BqFWHU1i7jlmQZm04A0FQ/9dafaa079d796779b33dbf8c7aad2a22/Photo_requirements_visa.pdf)

## Processing Time
The standard processing time for visa processing is 15 calendar days.
The processing time might be extended for citizens of certain countries or specific categories of travelers due to mandatory visa consultation.                                                                                 Regular invitation letter issued by the host person (not registered at "Urząd Wojewódzki") must indicate personal data of the inviting and invited parties, the purpose and
                                                                                duration of the visit, the place of residence of the applicant, relations between the host and applicant, a written commitment to cover the applicant’s living, return and
                                                                                healthcare costs if necessary, signature of the inviting party.

                                                                                costs associated with the applicant’s stay as well as a document confirming the host’s legal title to the residential premises or a document confirming the host’s ability
                                                                                of providing accommodation to the applicant elsewhere, e.g. an ownership act, rent contract, reservation in the hotel.
- - Documents proving family ties with apostille stamp or legalized by the competent authorities issued within the past 6 months
The certification must be submitted only if the applicant and the host person are relatives, e.g. marital status, birth certificates, etc.
- Marriage certificate certified with apostille stamp or legalized by the competent authorities issued within the past 6 months
If family member of Polish/EU/EEA citizens only.
- Travel health insurance certificate and its copy
The sum insured has to exceed 30.000 Euro.
Only a policy issued by an insurance company based in the EU or listed insurance companies recommended for national visa are accepted by the Embassy of Poland in Manila.
The policy shall be valid for all Schengen area states.
The policy shall cover at least the entire period of stay.

- Confirmation of sufficient financial means for the applicant
e.g. bank statements presenting the transactions / movements of the last 6 months or the tax clearance for the previous year.
Please note that officially registered invitation ("Zaproszenie") may confirm financial means for the applicant. Nevertheless presentation of regular income does prove ties with
                                                                                the country and may serve as one of the guarantors of return before the visa expiration.
- Confirmation of sufficient financial means for the host person / guarantor
e.g. notary sponsorship letter covering the period of the requested visa, bank statements presenting the transactions / movements of the last 6 months or tax clearance for the
                                                                                previous year. Please note that a document proving financial means is not required when officially registered invitation is presented.
- Confirmation of employment / private business / studies / pension
For company employees - original letter from employer confirming the position in the company, date of employment, salary for the last 6 months and period of holidays granted (certificate
                                                                                of leave of absence). The letter must include the name and position of the person who signed the certificate, registration and contact details of the company.
For private business – confirmation of business registration, license, income tax return, business financial statement;
For students – proof of enrolment, certificate from the school with confirmation of leave of absence and the valid student's ID card. You may be also requested to submit a student’s
                                                                                record-book;
For university representatives – letter from the school confirming employment and purpose of travelling;
For pensioners – pension certificate indicating the amount of pension;
For unemployed – a written and signed explanation on the applicant’s labour situation;
- (if applicable)
- - Hotel reservation or other proof of accommodation
Please note that only officially registered invitation confirms place of accommodation. Other cases require hotel reservation or written statement of the host on the character
                                                                                and location of accommodation.
- Birth certificate, notarized consent from custodian/certificate of custody/letter from school/a copy of a custodian’s passport/Department of Social Welfare and Development clearance
For children under 18 years of age only.
- Certificate from school
If child is applying.
- Copy of Alien Certificate Registration (ACR)/re-entry permit - the document shall be valid for at least 3 months after expiry of the visa.
For residents holding non-Philippine passport only.

visa_types.name: REFUSAL, ANNULMENT, REVOCATION OF A VISA
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The applications for reconsideration of negative visa decision should be submitted (personally or by an authorized person) at the Poland Visa Application Centre between 9.00 am -12.00 noon, Monday through Friday except for Polish and Philippine holidays. There is no need for online appointment. The fee for an appeal is the same as for the original application.
The original of written appeal is to be lodged within 14 days of receipt of the negative visa decision.
NATIONAL VISA (D-TYPE)
In the case of refusal to issue a national visa, the decision is presented to the applicant on a standard form, constituting Annex 3 to the Ordinance of the Minister of Interior and Administration of April 19, 2019 on visas for foreigners (Journal of Laws of 2019, item 782). The form shall state the reasons for the decision.
In the case of annulment / revocation of a national visa, the decision is presented on a standard form attached to the Ordinance of the Minister of the Interior on annulment and revocation of visas for foreigners of April 28, 2014 (Journal of Laws of 2014, item 560). The form specifies the reasons for the decision to annul / revoke the visa.
SCHENGEN VISA (C-TYPE)
In the case of refusal to issue a Schengen visa and its annulment and revocation, the decision is presented to the applicant on the standard form constituting Annex VI to the Regulation of the European Parliament and of the Council (EC) No. 810/2009 of July 13, 2009 establishing a Community Code on Visas (Visa Code) of 13 July 2009 (Official Journal of the European Union 2009 No. 243). The form specifies the reasons for the decision.
APPEAL PROCEDURE
Persons who have been refused a visa or received a decision to annul / revoke a visa have the right to appeal. Appeals for both national and Schengen visas are conducted against the Member State that made the decision and in accordance with the national law of that State.
A decision issued by a consul of the Republic of Poland may be requested for the case to be reconsidered by this authority. The application for reconsideration of the case shall be submitted within 14 days from the date of delivery of the decision on refusal / annulment / revocation of the visa.
The fee for an appeal is the same as for the original application EUR 80.
Consular fee in accordance with the Table of Consular Fees (amount in PHP peso equivalent to EUR 80).
The consul examines the application for reconsideration of the case within 14 working days of its submission.
According to Art. 94 sec. 3 of the Act of 25 June 2015 Consular Law (Journal of Laws of 2015, item 1274), in the case of national visas, the decision made in the appeal procedure is final. If, after examining the application for reconsideration, the consul issued another decision to refuse, annul or revoke the Schengen visa, with which the applicant does not agree, in this case, a complaint may be lodged with the Provincial Administrative Court in Warsaw.

visa_types.documents_required:
- Registered application form
Form must be filled out on-line, printed out and signed personally by the applicant (for minors, the application shall be signed personally by at least one of the parents or a
                                                                                legal guardian while submitting the application).

- 1 passport size colour photograph
Size 35 x 45 mm.
White background.

Printed on high quality paper.

- Original passport
Must be valid at least 3 months calculating from the date of intended departure from the Schengen area.
Must contain at least two blank visa pages, excluding the endorsements pages.
Not older than 10 years (extended passports are not accepted).
- - - - Preliminary employment agreement (original in and its photocopy) (if available)
The preliminary employment agreement must stipulate place, conditions and character of employment. The document shall be signed by both parties and drawn up in Polish and English.
- Written declaration from the employer
The letter should confirm the intention to employ the foreigner as well as the character of employment, the position and the salary of the employee. The document must also include the contact details of the employer as well as the statement on providing the employee with social security scheme in Poland at the actual start of performing employment duties.
- Proof of accommodation
e.g. confirmation letter from the employer confirming providing accommodation with indication of its character and location (dormitory, apartment), hotel reservation.
- Documents proving the scope of activities of the employer
e.g. the company registration, documents confirming the income, amount of the income tax and the number of persons employed.
- Health insurance (original and its photocopy)
Insurance certificate must cover the entire period of the visa validity.
The sum insured has to exceed 30.000 Euro.
Only a policy issued by an insurance company based in the EU or listed insurance companies recommended for national visa are accepted by the Embassy of Poland in Manila.
The policy shall be valid for the Schengen Member States.
List of Schengen Insurance Companies (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6grCRUd0t5zxTXa84TeHME/92037f344e34bc81b5988d3df58eedd9/list-of-schengen-insurance-companies-20-oct-2022.pdf)
- - - - - Confirmation of sufficient financial means necessary to cover the costs of foreigner’s entry into and at least the first month of stay in the territory of the Republic of Poland.
e.g. bank statements presenting the transactions / movements of the last 6 months.
*IMPORTANT NOTE:
Visas for the purpose of carrying out work are issued by the Polish Consul at the office relevant to the applicant's legal residence. The Polish consulate may also accept an application for employment visa lodged by a third-country national legally present but not residing in its jurisdiction, if the applicant has provided justification for lodging the application at that consulate. This is a Schengen visa (stay up to 90 days) or national type of visa issued for the period of stay corresponding to the period specified in the work permit but not exceeding one year.
Employment without prior authorization and relevant visa is strictly prohibited.

                                                                                legal guardian while submitting the application).
All entries should be properly filled out - no blanks.
- 1 passport size colour photograph
Size 35 x 45 mm.
White background.
Not older than 6 months.
Printed on high quality paper.
Complying with ICAO guidelines (International Civil Aviation Organization)
- Original passport
Must be valid at least 3 months calculating from the date of intended departure from the Schengen area.
Must contain at least two blank visa pages, excluding the endorsements pages.
Not older than 10 years (extended passports are not accepted).
- - - - Preliminary employment agreement (original in and its photocopy) (if available)
The preliminary employment agreement must stipulate place, conditions and character of employment. The document shall be signed by both parties and drawn up in Polish and English.
- Written declaration from the employer
The letter should confirm the intention to employ the foreigner as well as the character of employment, the position and the salary of the employee. The document must also include the contact details of the employer as well as the statement on providing the employee with social security scheme in Poland at the actual start of performing employment duties.
- Proof of accommodation
e.g. confirmation letter from the employer confirming providing accommodation with indication of its character and location (dormitory, apartment), hotel reservation.
- Documents proving the scope of activities of the employer
e.g. the company registration, documents confirming the income, amount of the income tax and the number of persons employed.
- Health insurance (original and its photocopy)
Insurance certificate must cover the entire period of the visa validity.
The sum insured has to exceed 30.000 Euro.
Only a policy issued by an insurance company based in the EU or listed insurance companies recommended for national visa are accepted by the Embassy of Poland in Manila.
The policy shall be valid for the Schengen Member States.

- - - - - Confirmation of sufficient financial means necessary to cover the costs of foreigner’s entry into and at least the first month of stay in the territory of the Republic of Poland.
e.g. bank statements presenting the transactions / movements of the last 6 months.
*IMPORTANT NOTE:
Visas for the purpose of carrying out work are issued by the Polish Consul at the office relevant to the applicant's legal residence. The Polish consulate may also accept an application for employment visa lodged by a third-country national legally present but not residing in its jurisdiction, if the applicant has provided justification for lodging the application at that consulate. This is a Schengen visa (stay up to 90 days) or national type of visa issued for the period of stay corresponding to the period specified in the work permit but not exceeding one year.
Employment without prior authorization and relevant visa is strictly prohibited.

## Photo Specifications
All photos must meet the requirements listed below. If these requirements have not been met, the applicant will have to deliver a new photo that meets the requirements.
FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
- Size of photograph: 3.5 X 4.5 cm
- Sharp and in color, printed on high-quality paper with a white background
- Not older than 6 months, enabling identification of applying person
- "En face" - face straight to the objective, both sides of the face need to be visible
- Photograph must present a person from top of head to shoulders
- Size of the head should cover 70-80% of vertical length of a photo
- Head must be uncovered and unveiled, face must not be covered by hair, eyes must be visible, mouth must be shut
IF YOU WEAR GLASSES:
- Glasses must not be dark
- Eyes must be visible through frames
- Glasses must not reflect the light
IF YOU WEAR RELIGIOUS HEAD COVERS:
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- Face must not be shadowed by head cover
The photo requirements are the same for children as for adults. 
Photo requirements visa (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5BqFWHU1i7jlmQZm04A0FQ/9dafaa079d796779b33dbf8c7aad2a22/Photo_requirements_visa.pdf)

The standard processing time for visa processing is 15 calendar days.

## Photo Specifications

- Not older than 6 months, enabling identification of applying person
- "En face" - face straight to the objective, both sides of the face need to be visible
- Photograph must present a person from top of head to shoulders
- Size of the head should cover 70-80% of vertical length of a photo
- Head must be uncovered and unveiled, face must not be covered by hair, eyes must be visible, mouth must be shut
IF YOU WEAR GLASSES:
- Glasses must not be dark
- Eyes must be visible through frames
- Glasses must not reflect the light
IF YOU WEAR RELIGIOUS HEAD COVERS:

- Face must not be shadowed by head cover

visa_types.documents_required:
- Registered application form
Form must be filled out on-line, printed out and signed personally by the applicant (for minors, the application shall be signed personally by at least one of the parents or a
                                                                                legal guardian while submitting the application).
All entries should be properly filled out - no blanks.
- 1 passport size colour photograph
Size 35 x 45 mm.
White background.
Not older than 6 months.
Printed on high quality paper.
Complying with ICAO guidelines (International Civil Aviation Organization)
- Original passport
Must be valid at least 3 months calculating from the date of intended departure from the Schengen area.
Must contain at least two blank visa pages, excluding the endorsements pages.
Not older than 10 years (extended passports are not accepted).
- - - Work permit (original and its photocopy)
Original document issued by relevant Polish local authorities must be presented.
- Preliminary employment agreement (original in and its photocopy) (if available)
The preliminary employment agreement must stipulate place, conditions and character of employment. The document shall be signed by both parties and drawn up in Polish and English.
- Written declaration from the employer
The letter should confirm the intention to employ the foreigner as well as the character of employment, the position and the salary of the employee. The document must also include
                                                                                the contact details of the employer as well as the statement on providing the employee with social security scheme in Poland at the actual start of performing employment duties.
- Proof of accommodation
e.g. confirmation letter from the employer confirming providing accommodation with indication of its character and location (dormitory, apartment), hotel reservation.
- Documents proving the scope of activities of the employer
e.g. the company registration, documents confirming the income, amount of the income tax and the number of persons employed.
- Health insurance (original and its photocopy)
Insurance certificate must cover the entire period of the visa validity.
The sum insured has to exceed 30.000 Euro.
Only a policy issued by an insurance company based in the EU or listed insurance companies recommended for national visa are accepted by the Embassy of Poland in Manila.
The policy shall be valid for the Schengen Member States.
List of Insurance Companies in The Philippines recommended for National Visa  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1oYsawFkjNKnonc2q90Bk5/79fa15c9e14f5f8c899be525cf5d83f8/list-of-insurance-companies-in-philippines-recommended-for-national-visa.pdf)
- - - - - Confirmation of sufficient financial means necessary to cover the costs of foreigner’s entry into and at least the first month of stay in the territory of the Republic of Poland.
e.g. bank statements presenting the transactions / movements of the last 6 months.
*IMPORTANT NOTE:
Visas for the purpose of carrying out work are issued by the Polish Consul at the office relevant to the applicant's legal residence . The Polish consulate may also accept an application
                                                                        for employment visa lodged by a third-country national legally present but not residing in its jurisdiction, if the applicant has provided justification for lodging the application
                                                                        at that consulate. This is a Schengen visa (stay up to 90 days) or national type of visa issued for the period of stay corresponding to the period specified in the work permit but
                                                                        not exceeding one year.
Employment without prior authorization and relevant visa is strictly prohibited.

visa_types.name: Applying for a national "D" visa
visa_types.overview:
It is issued to persons who intend to stay in the territory of Poland jointly (during one or several entries) for more than 90 days within a year from the date of the first entry.
A long-stay national visa (D) authorizes its holder to stay:
- On the territory of Poland within the validity period for which it was issued;
- Within the territory of the other Schengen states for up to 90 days in each 180-day period calculated from the date of entry into the territory of the Member States.
The period of validity of a national visa may not exceed 1 year.
Persons applying for a national "D" visa are required to book their appointment through our VFS online booking systemhttps://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/pol/login (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/pol/login)
To acquire a registered application form kindly visit the Embassy online system https://secure2.e-konsulat.gov.pl/ (https://secure2.e-konsulat.gov.pl/)                                                            and follow instructions on the website. Please choose National Visa - Fill in Form. Complete it, click on “Save”, and print out the application form and bring it with you to the VFS Poland Visa Application Centre (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/pol/attend-centre/makati) in Makati for submission.
LIST OF MANDATORY DOCUMENTS FOR SCHENGEN VISA APPLICANTS
- Visa application from – completed in Polish or English and signed.
A visa application for a minor must be submitted and signed by one of the parents or legal guardian. This means that a separate visa application form must be submitted for the minor, entered
                                                                    in the passport of the parent or legal guardian who receives a separate visa for the passport of the parent or legal guardian.
- Current color photo
- Size 35 x 45 mm.
- White background.
- Not older than 6 months.
- Printed on high quality paper.
- Please verify the requirements (Visa information → Photograph).
- Travel document and a copy of the first page of the passport.
- The passport must be signed and valid for at least 3 months after the expiry of the visa.
- The validity of the passport may not exceed 10 years.
- Extended passports will not be accepted.
- Passport should contain at least 2 blank pages (for visas).
- Passports are accepted without additional plastic or leather wrappers, etc.
- If you have other foreign passports, please attach them to the visa application (original and photocopy).
- Documents confirming the purpose and conditions of the planned stay in the territory of the Republic of Poland.
- Documents proving sufficient financial resources to cover travel costs.
They can be delivered in the form of:
- Bank statement for the last 6 months.
- Signed traveler’s checks.
- Credit cards (a letter from the bank confirming the possession of a credit card and card limit).
- Certificate from the workplace with the position and salary during the last 6 months.
- Invitation (original and photocopy) issued pursuant to the Act of June 13, 2003 on foreigners (confirmed by the Voivodship Office).
- Documents confirming the profession and earned income as proof of the applicant's financial relations with the country of residence:
- Employed persons: employment certificate confirming the position of the employee, date of employment and duration of leave. The letter must include the amount of remuneration, the name
                                                                        and position of the signatory and employer’s contact details.
- Private entrepreneurs: license / company registration; last tax declaration.
- Pupils and students: student ID or certificate from the school / university confirming the status of the student / student along with the consent to leave/ proof of enrolment / You may
                                                                        be also requested to submit a student’s record-book.;
- Documents confirming the ownership (land, house or, if applicable, other property - where applicable) or marital status, e.g. marriage certificate certified with apostille stamp or legalized
                                                                by the competent authorities.
- Health insurance – typed out:
- With minimum coverage of EUR 30,000
- Valid throughout the territory of all Schengen States.
- Covering the entire period of validity of the visa and all days of the planned stay.
Only a policy issued by an insurance company based in the EU or listed insurance companies recommended for national visa are accepted by the Embassy of Poland. 
You can download the list of accredited insurance companies in the Philippines recommended for National Visa (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1oYsawFkjNKnonc2q90Bk5/79fa15c9e14f5f8c899be525cf5d83f8/list-of-insurance-companies-in-philippines-recommended-for-national-visa.pdf) here
Information of the Minister of Foreign Affairs about the insurers and the insurances that they offer, which meet the necessary conditions referred to in Article 25 (1) (2) (a) and Article
                                                                    25 (1b) of the Act of 12 December 2013 on Foreigners is available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (https://www.gov.pl/web/diplomacy/visas).
- Free flight / travel ticket reservations or other proof of the intended means of transportation.
- For minors traveling without or only with one parent: written consent from the other parent / guardian is required (original and photocopy). In cases where such consent is impossible to obtain
                                                                - documents explaining the reason for such a lack (a statement confirming the status of the sole parent or other documents). Birth certificate certified with apostille stamp or legalized
                                                                by the competent authorities (original and photocopy). Copies of passports of parents or guardians and copies of valid visas in case of joint travel.
- A residence permit under the law of the Republic of the Philippines along with the right of re-entry (in the case of foreigners residing in the Republic of the Philippines).
LIST OF SUPPLEMENTARY DOCUMENTS TO BE PRESENTED BY MINORS
- A birth certificate certified with apostille stamp or legalized by the competent authorities;
- A notarized consent of a parent or legal guardian who did not sign the visa application for the minor's departure from the Philippines (original and copy). If both parents / legal guardians are
                                                                present, a notarized consent to leave is not required when submitting a visa application from a minor;
- If a minor travels without both parents (for example with a sports group) – consent of both parents for the minor's departure from the Philippines drawn up by a notary;
- If a minor has only one guardian, an appropriate document should be presented (a court decision granting sole parental authority, a death certificate or another document);
- In the case of minors traveling with accompanying parents, a photocopy of the valid parental visa is required.
IMPORTANT ADVISORY
- Applicants must present two (2) sets of documents for a visa application – one (1) original set of documents and one (1) set of photocopies. 
All documents must be arranged according to the checklist of supporting documents required for visa application process.
- All documents for the set of photocopies should be in A4 size paper.
- Applicants are requested to submit the required documents during the visa application.
- The checklists of documents are not exhaustive.
The Consul may request each applicant to provide additional documents confirming the information provided in the visa application and ask the applicant to come to the Embassy in person for
                                                                    an interview.
- All forms of communication to the applicant for request of additional documents or further information on the status of their application will be sent through the provided email address on the
                                                                form. Therefore, applicants are advised to regularly check their emails for messages during the whole period of processing of their visa applications.
- For family and group applications, each applicant must have the same set of documents and also arranged according to the checklist of supporting documents required for visa application process.

It is issued to persons who intend to stay in the territory of Poland jointly (during one or several entries) for more than 90 days within a year from the date of the first entry.
A long-stay national visa (D) authorizes its holder to stay:
- On the territory of Poland within the validity period for which it was issued;
- Within the territory of the other Schengen states for up to 90 days in each 180-day period calculated from the date of entry into the territory of the Member States.
The period of validity of a national visa may not exceed 1 year.
Persons applying for a national "D" visa are required to book their appointment through our VFS online booking systemhttps://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/pol/login (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/pol/login)
To acquire a registered application form kindly visit the Embassy online system https://secure2.e-konsulat.gov.pl/ (https://secure2.e-konsulat.gov.pl/)                                                            and follow instructions on the website. Please choose National Visa - Fill in Form. Complete it, click on “Save”, and print out the application form and bring it with you to the VFS Poland Visa Application Centre (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/pol/attend-centre/makati) in Makati for submission.
LIST OF MANDATORY DOCUMENTS FOR SCHENGEN VISA APPLICANTS
- Visa application from – completed in Polish or English and signed.
A visa application for a minor must be submitted and signed by one of the parents or legal guardian. This means that a separate visa application form must be submitted for the minor, entered
                                                                    in the passport of the parent or legal guardian who receives a separate visa for the passport of the parent or legal guardian.
- Current color photo
- Size 35 x 45 mm.
- White background.
- Not older than 6 months.
- Printed on high quality paper.
- Please verify the requirements (Visa information → Photograph).
- Travel document and a copy of the first page of the passport.
- The passport must be signed and valid for at least 3 months after the expiry of the visa.
- The validity of the passport may not exceed 10 years.
- Extended passports will not be accepted.
- Passport should contain at least 2 blank pages (for visas).
- Passports are accepted without additional plastic or leather wrappers, etc.
- If you have other foreign passports, please attach them to the visa application (original and photocopy).
- Documents confirming the purpose and conditions of the planned stay in the territory of the Republic of Poland.
- Documents proving sufficient financial resources to cover travel costs.
They can be delivered in the form of:
- Bank statement for the last 6 months.
- Signed traveler’s checks.
- Credit cards (a letter from the bank confirming the possession of a credit card and card limit).
- Certificate from the workplace with the position and salary during the last 6 months.
- Invitation (original and photocopy) issued pursuant to the Act of June 13, 2003 on foreigners (confirmed by the Voivodship Office).
- Documents confirming the profession and earned income as proof of the applicant's financial relations with the country of residence:
- Employed persons: employment certificate confirming the position of the employee, date of employment and duration of leave. The letter must include the amount of remuneration, the name
                                                                        and position of the signatory and employer’s contact details.
- Private entrepreneurs: license / company registration; last tax declaration.
- Pupils and students: student ID or certificate from the school / university confirming the status of the student / student along with the consent to leave/ proof of enrolment / You may
                                                                        be also requested to submit a student’s record-book.;
- Documents confirming the ownership (land, house or, if applicable, other property - where applicable) or marital status, e.g. marriage certificate certified with apostille stamp or legalized
                                                                by the competent authorities.
- Health insurance – typed out:
- With minimum coverage of EUR 30,000
- Valid throughout the territory of all Schengen States.
- Covering the entire period of validity of the visa and all days of the planned stay.
Only a policy issued by an insurance company based in the EU or listed insurance companies recommended for national visa are accepted by the Embassy of Poland. 
You can download the list of accredited insurance companies in the Philippines recommended for National Visa (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1oYsawFkjNKnonc2q90Bk5/79fa15c9e14f5f8c899be525cf5d83f8/list-of-insurance-companies-in-philippines-recommended-for-national-visa.pdf) here
Information of the Minister of Foreign Affairs about the insurers and the insurances that they offer, which meet the necessary conditions referred to in Article 25 (1) (2) (a) and Article
                                                                    25 (1b) of the Act of 12 December 2013 on Foreigners is available on the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (https://www.gov.pl/web/diplomacy/visas).
- Free flight / travel ticket reservations or other proof of the intended means of transportation.
- For minors traveling without or only with one parent: written consent from the other parent / guardian is required (original and photocopy). In cases where such consent is impossible to obtain
                                                                - documents explaining the reason for such a lack (a statement confirming the status of the sole parent or other documents). Birth certificate certified with apostille stamp or legalized
                                                                by the competent authorities (original and photocopy). Copies of passports of parents or guardians and copies of valid visas in case of joint travel.
- A residence permit under the law of the Republic of the Philippines along with the right of re-entry (in the case of foreigners residing in the Republic of the Philippines).

- If a minor travels without both parents (for example with a sports group) – consent of both parents for the minor's departure from the Philippines drawn up by a notary;
- If a minor has only one guardian, an appropriate document should be presented (a court decision granting sole parental authority, a death certificate or another document);
- In the case of minors traveling with accompanying parents, a photocopy of the valid parental visa is required.
